response:
Brittany uses 1/4 ounce of shampoo every other day, which means she uses 1/2 ounce per week. In a leap year, there are 366 days, so she will use shampoo for 183 days. Multiplying 183 days by 1/4 ounce gives a total of 45.75 ounces of shampoo needed for the year. Since she buys shampoo in 14-ounce bottles, she will need to calculate how many bottles are required to cover this amount.

[Number of bottles] is calculated by dividing the total shampoo needed by the size of each bottle. 45.75 ounces divided by 14 ounces per bottle equals approximately 3.27 bottles. Since she cannot purchase a fraction of a bottle, she will need to round up to the nearest whole number, which is [number of bottles]. This ensures she has enough shampoo to last through the entire leap year.
